# emu/wii — Dolphin assets

Everything in this directory except this README is **git-ignored**. Drop your
own files here; `tools/setup_wii.sh` will look for them and wire up a Dolphin
config.

## Required files

| File | What it is | Where to get it |
|---|---|---|
| `Dolphin.app` | Dolphin emulator (macOS build) | https://dolphin-emu.org/download/ |

Dolphin needs **no copyrighted BIOS** to boot homebrew `.dol` files, so unlike
xemu there's nothing to dump. A NAND backup (`nand/`) is optional and only
matters if you want the System Menu / your own save data.

## Generated by setup / run

| File | What it is |
|---|---|
| `~/Library/Application Support/Dolphin/Config/Dolphin.ini` | rewritten by `tools/setup_wii.sh`; original backed up to `.relic-bak` |
| `dist/wii/sd/apps/relic/` | virtual SD card root, synced into the guest at boot |

## Workflow

```sh
tools/setup_wii.sh        # one-time: drop Dolphin.app first
make run-wii              # build + launch, every time
```

## Input / networking

- `run_wii.sh` plugs an emulated **GameCube keyboard** into controller
  port 1 (`SIDevice0 = 7`); Relic reads it over SI. (Dolphin's separate
  Wii-USB-keyboard emulation is Windows-only, so SI is the path that
  works on macOS/Linux.) On real hardware plug any USB keyboard into a
  back port — `wii_kbd.c` reads `/dev/usb/kbd` too.
- **Known macOS quirk:** Dolphin's Quartz input backend reports keycode 0
  (the `A` key) as permanently held; Relic filters the ghost so it
  doesn't auto-type, but pressing host `A` still won't register. Grant
  Dolphin **Input Monitoring** in System Settings → Privacy & Security
  (or rebind `Keys/A` in Controllers → Port 1 → Configure) if `a` is
  dead.
- `SerialPort1 = 10` enables the emulated **Broadband Adapter (HLE)**, which
  routes the guest's TCP through the host stack — no extra config needed.
